evals.name: list-with-key-and-filter
evals.prompt: Render a list of active products in a template, but only the ones where product.active is true

assertions.text: Uses v-for with a mandatory stable :key (e.g. product.id), never combines v-if and v-for on the same element, and filters via a computed property rather than v-if inside the loop

evals.name: native-vmodel-modifiers
evals.prompt: I have a plain HTML email input and an age number input. How do I two-way bind them and clean up the values?

assertions.text: Uses v-model.trim on the email input and v-model.number on the age input, with refs as the source of truth; mentions native v-model expands to value + input for text inputs

evals.name: event-and-class-binding
evals.prompt: On a button I want to prevent the default form submit, toggle an 'is-open' class when a ref is true, and disable it while loading

assertions.text: Uses @submit.prevent (or @click with .prevent), object syntax :class="{ 'is-open': isOpen }", and a boolean :disabled binding; uses @ and : shorthands

evals.name: negative-vuetify-form-validation
evals.prompt: Build a Vuetify text field with required and email validation rules and show the error message

assertions.text: Routes to the vuetify-components skill for v-text-field and :rules validation rather than answering with native v-model; this skill covers native HTML inputs only
